question: Will real GDP increase by more than 2.0% in Q1 2026?
reasoning: Current nowcast models show Q1 2026 GDP growth significantly below the 2.0% threshold. The Atlanta Fed's GDPNow has declined to 1.3% as of April 7, down from 1.6% in early April. While the New York Fed nowcast is higher at 2.4%, the weight of evidence points to growth below 2.0%. Q4 2025 GDP was weak at just 1.4% (revised down from earlier estimates), and recent data on consumer spending and retail sales has been soft, contributing to the downward revisions in nowcasts.
key_factors: Atlanta Fed GDPNow model at 1.3% for Q1 2026 (down from 1.6%)
New York Fed nowcast at 2.4% provides some upside risk
Q4 2025 GDP weak at 1.4%, showing economic momentum loss
Soft consumer spending and retail sales data driving downgrades
Market resolution based on BEA advance estimate on April 30
risks: New York Fed nowcast at 2.4% suggests potential for 2%+ growth
Final two weeks before April 30 release could see data surprises
Geopolitical tensions (Iran war) creating economic uncertainty
Nowcast models can have significant forecast errors near quarter-end
